back_translation: Therefore, let us leave behind the elementary principles of the teaching of Christ, and press on toward maturity, not laying again a foundation in the matter of repentance from dead works, and faith in God,
that is, the teaching about baptisms, the laying on of hands, the rising up from death, and eternal judgment.
And we will do thus, if God permits.
For it is impossible to bring back those people, who once received light, who tasted the heavenly gift, and have become participants in the Holy Spirit,
and tasted the good word of God, and the powers of the age that is to come,
and then went astray — to bring them back again to repentance, because they themselves crucify the Son of God on the cross again, and put Him to public shame.
For ground that drinks the rain that falls upon it often, and produces useful plants for those who cultivate it, receives a blessing from God.
But if it produces thorn-bushes and thorn-wood, it is unusable and near a curse; its end is to be burned.
But we are confident concerning you, beloved ones, of better things, and things that go together with salvation, even though we speak in this way.
For God is not unjust to forget your work, and the love which you have shown toward His name, in that you have served the holy ones and continue to serve.
And we desire that each of you show the same diligence, so that hope may be made full and complete to the very end.
so that you all will not become sluggish people, but be imitators of those who, through faith and patience, receive the inheritance of the promises.
For when God gave a promise to Abraham, since He had no one greater than Himself by whom He could swear, He swore by Himself,
saying, "I will surely bless you, and I will surely multiply you abundantly."
And in this way, when Abraham had patiently waited, he received what was promised.
For human beings swear by one greater than themselves, and the oath is a confirmation that ends all their disputes.
In this case, God desired to show even more to the heirs of the promise the unchangeableness of His purpose, so He guaranteed it with an oath,
so that by two unchangeable things, in which it is impossible for God to lie, we who have fled for refuge may have strong encouragement, to hold firmly to the hope set before us.
This hope is like an anchor for our soul, firm and certain, and it enters into the inner part of the curtain,
where Jesus, who leads ahead for our sake, has entered, having become a high priest forever, in the order of Melchizedek.
